instructions: Baton is a safety-sensitive Go CLI plus bundled Codex skill for reusable GitHub issue and PR agent workflows. Deterministic policy, git, GitHub, queue, install, branch, and work-item transition logic belongs in the CLI; execution completion and checkout isolation belong to the caller; agent judgment belongs in the skill. Reviews should prioritize correctness, explicit error handling, dry-run or pure planning paths for mutations, and preservation of target repository policy as the source of truth.

rules.rule: Flag automation paths that can mutate a user's primary checkout. Repository mutation should happen only inside caller-provided isolated execution context unless the code is explicitly read-only.
Every command that mutates GitHub or git state should expose a dry-run path or pure planner that can be tested without side effects.
Flag attempts to add worktree leasing, pruning, deletion, reset, or cleanup lifecycle back into Baton. Checkout lifecycle belongs to Coda, Treehouse, Codex, or the user.
Flag PR merge behavior unless the code or skill guidance requires an explicit user merge request and verifies the target repository policy allows the merge.
GitHub Actions policy workflows must run trusted Baton code, not code modified by an untrusted PR.
Command behavior should be driven by structured result objects and JSON-first contracts. Human text output can wrap those same results, but must not become the source of truth.
Policy parsing and decision logic should have focused table-driven tests that cover GitHub event fixtures, edge cases, and denied unsafe states.
